hero.name: Kelsey
hero.quote: Entering impossible rooms. Building trust. Finding gaps. Executing solutions.

narrative.title: About
narrative.highlight: I enter rooms others consider impossible, build trust in complex environments, and execute strategies that move the needle.
narrative.body:
My path from Goldman Sachs institutional client management to emergency medical training to medical device sales isn't random—it's a deliberate runway toward a General Partner role in healthcare AI venture capital.

I've built a career on pattern recognition: finding where the friction is, understanding what's actually needed, and designing solutions that work. Whether managing institutional relationships at Goldman, building a territory from zero as a missionary representative, or providing real clinical care in emergency departments, the throughline is the same: enter with respect, listen for the gap, solve the problem.

I'm now applying that same discipline to medical sales—using my clinical credibility and institutional background to navigate the complex world of healthcare technology adoption. The goal is clear: prove the model, build the network, and position myself for the VC side of healthcare innovation.

experience.title: Medical Sales Coaching Student
experience.body: Structured 6-month intensive focused on Intuitive Surgical, Stryker, and Medtronic opportunities. Completed STAR(T) stories, portfolio development, and landed confirmed calls with hiring managers.
bullets: Completed 8+ STAR(T) stories documenting leadership, resilience, and results
Developed a custom portfolio website demonstrating clinical + sales narrative
Secured confirmed calls with multiple hiring managers in medical device

experience.title: Licensed EMT — Clinical Experience
experience.body: Earned EMT licensure after intensive training; currently gaining real-world clinical experience in high-acuity emergency settings.
bullets: Ranked in top 7% of EMT class in theory and practical assessment
Direct exposure to trauma, emergency protocols, and interdisciplinary clinical teams
Building clinical credibility for medical device/healthcare AI narrative

experience.title: Global Markets Analyst
experience.body: Managed client relationships for institutional accounts, built forecasts, and identified market opportunities.
bullets: Managed $500M+ in client assets across equities and fixed income
Identified and sized a $2M+ opportunity, built and sold the business case to senior leadership
Built forecasts in SQL, Snowflake, and Python aligned to board-level targets

experience.title: Spanish-Speaking Representative — Nebraska, Iowa & South Dakota
experience.body: Managed a Spanish-speaking territory across 3 states. Built and maintained a pipeline tracking system increasing referral growth and reducing cold prospecting.
bullets: Grew territory pipeline 40% through systematic prospecting and follow-up
Trained incoming representative while maintaining performance targets
Developed rapport in culturally diverse markets with limited English-speaking resources

recommendations.label: EMT INSTRUCTOR
recommendations.name: Aditya Kanakasabapathy
recommendations.title: Clinical Skills Instructor, UT Dallas
recommendations.text: Kelsey demonstrated exceptional clinical acuity and the ability to stay calm under pressure. She earned the highest marks in her EMT class and showed the kind of attention to detail and human-centered thinking that translates directly to patient care.

recommendations.label: TERRITORY MANAGER
recommendations.name: Justin Atchison
recommendations.title: Territory Manager, iRhythm Technologies
recommendations.text: What stands out about Kelsey is her ability to navigate complex institutional environments and build credibility quickly. In medical device, that's everything—she has both the clinical foundation and the relationship-building instinct that hiring managers are looking for.

recommendations.label: STRYKER CONNECTION
recommendations.name: Brock Frazer
recommendations.title: Senior Sales Leader, Stryker
recommendations.text: Kelsey has the rare combination of clinical credibility, institutional sales experience, and the discipline to execute at scale. She's exactly the kind of person who can bridge the gap between hospital operations and device adoption.

recommendations.label: SMARTCARE ADVOCATE
recommendations.name: Spencer Curtis
recommendations.title: Family Friend & Healthcare Entrepreneur
recommendations.text: Kelsey's ability to master complex systems and build relationships across different stakeholder groups is rare. In the healthcare AI space, that's the critical skill set. I'd back her play on Intuitive or Stryker without hesitation.

contact.title: Let's Connect
contact.headline: I'd love to hear from you.
contact.tagline: "Solving for the person across from me."
